rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Use only visible AADL features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation-level AGREE assignments.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes depending on whether they describe structural or behavioral properties.
Generated AGREE code should not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed model.
When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with the types of their expressions.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use only assign for output bindings.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants and equations in AGREE must use scalar types: int, real, bool.
Assignments in implementation scopes must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Unresolved references in AGREE diagnostics should be traced to root causes in the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE that are not declared in the AADL model.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation-level AGREE scopes.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables.
